It also ties to perception (epistemological.perception) and common intensification (resonance.superficial). Lower scores reflect its role in amplifying size across individual, societal, and aesthetic dimensions.

It also connects to sensory perception (epistemological.perception), predictable presence (lawfulness.predictable), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic).

The word 'titanic' strongly resonates with material existence (8) as it directly describes physical largeness. Its essential nature (7) reflects its core meaning of bigness. Perception (7) is high because size is sensory evident. Catalytic (4) and structural (3) scores reflect how large entities can influence systems and embody foundational scale. Aesthetic (4) acknowledges the awe of grand size. Lower scores reflect its lack of direct ties to ethics, cosmic scale, or existential resonance.

The term 'too large' strongly resonates with physical materiality (ontological.material) and its destructive excess (symmetry.destructive). Its ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) and predictable consequences (lawfulness.predictable) further ground its resonance.
